gaps.description: The Bill of Rights is entirely a scaffold with placeholder text ('[To be defined by human principal]'). No substantive articles exist to define the enterprise scope (Article I), prohibited activities (Article II), core identity (Article III), human authority reservation (Article IV), amendment protocol (Article V), or interpretation rules (Article VI). As a result, the constitutional identity of the DIO is undefined, and the Arbiter (#190) cannot enforce any scope boundary. This renders the first compliance gate (BoR perimeter) inoperative, leaving the organism with no enforceable constitutional limits on action proposals.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-001",
      "priority": "critical",
      "section": "MSP + BoR",
      "specific_reference": "MSP Rules (none), BoR Articles I–VI (empty)",
      "gap_description": "The DIO lacks any operational constitution or strategic objectives. The Mission Statement Protocol is an empty seed with no rules, and the Bill of Rights is a scaffold with no defined scope, prohibitions, identity, or human authority reservation. Nomos has nothing to enforce beyond a default-permissive stance.",
      "grounding": "MSP Purpose section states it 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules.' With both documents empty, no translation occurs, leaving the DIO entirely ungoverned.",
      "impact": "All agentic activity operates without strategic boundaries, safety invariants, or priority programs. Memory formation, context retention, and dream-cycle operations proceed without alignment to any mission, causing drift, scope creep, and potential unsafe behavior. Compliance gate (MSP + genome) is vacuous.",
      "urgency": "high",
      "criticality": "critical"
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-002",
      "priority": "high",
      "section": "MSP",
      "specific_reference": "MSP strategic objectives (implied by purpose); Radiant dream-cycle health stats",
      "gap_description": "Radiant dream-cycle scheduling is disabled, with 'health: never_run' and 'last_dream: null' recorded in the radiant stats block, despite evidence of prior human-initiated dream cycles. Autonomous memory consolidation and context freshness are not operational.",
      "grounding": "MSP is intended to set strategic objectives for organs. Memory/context formation is a core operational objective implied by the existence of Radiant and dream cycles. Without an active, scheduled dream cycle, the DIO cannot maintain memory coherence or self-optimize.",
      "impact": "Degraded memory consolidation leads to stale context, redundant storage, and loss of institutional knowledge over time. The system cannot meet the strategic requirement of persistent, evolving intelligence. Embedding coverage at 72% further indicates incomplete semantic indexing.",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "criticality": "high"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-01",
      "title": "BoR Undefined – No Operational Compliance Boundaries",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights (BoR) remains a scaffold without substantive content; all articles (I–VI) are empty placeholders. The Arbiter (#190) verifies the BoR at every scope query, but no enforceable scope, prohibited activities, core identity, or human authority reservations exist. The MSP is strictly subordinate to the BoR, making compliance adjudication impossible. Without a ratified BoR, the DIO operates in an unconstrained default state where no enterprise perimeter can be enforced, and the entire constitutional safety layer is inoperative.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "Enterprise scope is unbounded; Arbiter scope queries fail or return empty, allowing any action to proceed. The DIO lacks its foundational reference for all Nomos compliance gates.",
      "grounds": [
        "BoR Article I – Scope of Enterprise (unpopulated)",
        "BoR Article II – Prohibited Activities (unpopulated)",
        "BoR Article III – Core Identity (unpopulated)",
        "BoR Article IV – Human Authority Reservation (unpopulated)",
        "BoR Article V – Amendment Protocol (unpopulated)",
        "BoR Article VI – Interpretation Rules (unpopulated)",
        "MSP Section: Relationship to the Bill of Rights (subordination clause)"
      ],
      "governance_state_evidence": "Governance State Slice is empty; no bor_version concept has been registered in Graph, and no governance-class state transitions reflect a ratified BoR."
    }
  ]
}
